what_it_does: Defines the token-driven CSS for a design-system UI: base reset, body chrome with gradients and noise, kind-coded node cards, status dots, buttons, and a responsive app-shell grid.
observations: Heavy use of CSS custom properties (var(--...)) for colors, radii, shadows, and kind accents.
Body background is a layered radial-gradient composition plus a fixed, pointer-events-none SVG fractal-noise overlay.
Node card (.node) is a 215px card with header bar, monogram chip, kind label, config pills, output area, address footer, and left/right ports.

Four data-kind attribute selectors exist (process, content, present, model), but comments explicitly mark only process and content as live node kinds; present/model are forward-provision/config-slot accents.
Status dot classes are limited to idle, ran, cached, stuck; s-await is defined but commented as an inbox/presence marker, not a node status.
Responsive layout uses a CSS grid .app with desktop three-column, tablet collapsible rail, and mobile bottom-tabbar/sheet behavior.
Font families include IBM Plex Sans/Mono and Bricolage Grotesque.
standouts: Guard comments inside CSS that forbid rendering certain data-kind values and status classes, making the stylesheet act partly as a design contract.
Complete responsive transformation from three-column desktop to mobile bottom-tab navigation.
Strict visual taxonomy linking node kinds to colored header bars, left spines, and monogram chips.
conventions: Token-driven styling: values live in tokens.json, looks live here.
Flat descendant selectors (.node .bar) rather than strict BEM.
Domain semantics (allowed node kinds, statuses) embedded as CSS comments.
Mixes component styling with app-shell layout and responsive rules in one file.
concerns: CSS rules for .node[data-kind=present] and .node[data-kind=model] are present despite comments saying those kinds should not appear on canvas nodes, risking dead code or misuse.
.s-await is styled even though the comment says it is not a node status, which could confuse consumers.
Mobile breakpoint hides .foot, but .foot is not defined in this file, leaving its role unclear.
Hard-coded sizes (215px, 1200px) coexist with token-driven values without fallbacks.
No fallback values for var(--...) references.
notes: Token-driven skin for node cards and the app shell, with explicit guards about which node kinds and statuses are semantically valid.
questions: Where are the referenced custom properties/tokens (e.g., --kind-process, --acc, --bg) defined?
What does the 'RHM' label in the mobile tab bar stand for?
What markup structure is expected inside .node (classes like .bar, .body, .cfg, .out, .addr)?
What is the role of the .foot element hidden on mobile?
How are data-kind values assigned and validated at runtime?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches for component styling, but the file also includes responsive app-shell layout rules that go beyond pure component looks.
intention_signals: To create a single visual source of truth for a node-graph canvas so that node kinds and engine states are instantly recognizable, while adapting the layout across desktop, tablet, and mobile.
novelty: CSS comments act as design-contract guards, explicitly distinguishing live node kinds and statuses from forward-provision or non-node concepts.
contribution: The visual vocabulary for node cards (kind colors, monogram chips, status dots, ports), the token/value separation pattern, and the responsive app-shell breakpoints.
summary_for_embedding: A CSS component stylesheet for a token-driven node-graph UI that styles the app shell, kind-coded node cards, status indicators, buttons, and responsive breakpoints.
intention_for_embedding: To centralize the look and layout of a node canvas application so that node kinds and engine states are instantly recognizable across devices.
